nativeRange.habitat: Shallow, slow-moving or seasonally flooded freshwater margins on Caribbean islands; the population popularized in the aquarium hobby was collected west of Havana, Cuba

decorPreferences: fine, smooth substrate to anchor delicate roots
open foreground with strong direct light

avoidWith: boisterous fish that uproot fine carpet plants

summary: Hemianthus callitrichoides 'Cuba' is the classic foreground carpet plant of high-tech planted tanks, with millimetre-sized bright-green leaves. Generally considered demanding: it tends to require strong light, pressurised CO2, and a nutrient-rich substrate to form a tight carpet.
careNotes: Hemianthus callitrichoides — known almost universally in the hobby as 'HC', 'HC Cuba', or 'Dwarf Baby Tears' — is a Caribbean (Cuban-endemic in trade terms) creeping herb popularised by Takashi Amano around 2003 and now considered a benchmark foreground carpet plant. Tropica currently lists the species under its newer accepted name Micranthemum callitrichoides, though the older Hemianthus callitrichoides name remains dominant in retail and hobby usage; the species sits in family Linderniaceae (older sources often still list Scrophulariaceae). It is among the smallest aquarium plants, with leaves only ~3–5 mm across on a carpet that typically stays 3–5 cm tall. Should not be confused with Hemianthus micranthemoides ('Pearl Weed'), which is a much larger stem plant. Care is consistently rated advanced: Tropica describes it as 'advanced', 2HR Aquarist reports roughly a 95% failure rate without CO2, and Buce Plant and aqua-fish rate it medium-to-difficult. Light is best at the high end (Tropica >0.5 W/L; bright lighting needed to keep the carpet compact rather than leggy). CO2 is strongly recommended, with Tropica citing roughly 15–25 mg/L; liquid carbon offers only limited success and is rarely enough on its own. Temperature 21–27 °C, pH 6.0–7.3, gH ~4–10 (low-to-moderately-soft water is preferred). A fine, smooth, nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil) with iron-rich enrichment and steady column dosing is generally advised; the tiny roots make planting fiddly, and a 'dry start' method is widely used to let the plant anchor before flooding. Once carpeted, trim every 2–3 weeks to keep the lower leaves illuminated — neglected carpets tend to detach and float up in sheets. The plant is sensitive to algae if light, CO2, nutrients, or maintenance slip out of balance, so stability matters more than aggressive optimisation. Often paired in iwagumi/nature-style layouts; Micranthemum 'Monte Carlo' is a frequently recommended less-demanding substitute when conditions can't be maintained.
breedingNotes: Propagation is vegetative: the carpet spreads horizontally by short runners, and aquascapers expand coverage by lifting a mature mat, dividing it into small clumps of a few stems each, and replanting them a few centimetres apart across the foreground. New growth with attached roots can also be trimmed and replanted. The dry start method (growing emersed on damp substrate under a sealed lid for 4–8 weeks before flooding) is widely used to establish a dense, well-rooted carpet before submerged conditions begin.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Tropica plant details for Micranthemum (Hemianthus) callitrichoides 'Cuba'. Provided height (3–5+ cm), high light demand (>0.5 W/L), high CO2 (~15–25 mg/L), medium growth rate, foreground placement, Cuban origin (west of Havana), and 'advanced' care rating. The originally supplied URL (.../Hemianthus-callitrichoides) 404'd; resolved to this versioned product page via Tropica's search.

additional.name: Modern Aquarium
additional.notes: Cross-checked carpet placement, mm-scale leaves, moderate-to-high light, CO2 strongly recommended, medium-to-fast growth, temperature 68–82 °F (~20–28 °C), Cuban origin, and the planting technique of separating a tissue-culture portion into individual plugs spaced apart for horizontal spread.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked family (listed under older Scrophulariaceae), origin (Cuba/North America), height ~1–2 in, medium-to-high light, CO2 required, pH 6.0–7.5, moderate-to-fast growth, medium-to-difficult care, and the dry-start recommendation for anchoring delicate roots.

additional.name: 2HR Aquarist
additional.notes: Used (via search snippet — page returned HTTP 403 to direct fetch) for the widely cited '95% failure rate without CO2' framing, the bright-lighting requirement for compact horizontal growth, the importance of fine nutrient-rich substrate with iron, and the trim-every-three-weeks maintenance cadence.

additional.name: Aqua-Fish.Net
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature 21–27 °C, pH 6.0–7.3, hardness 5–11 dGH, maximum height 0–5 cm, foreground carpeting placement, propagation by runners, and substrate/iron fertilisation guidance. Used to average water parameters with Buce Plant (pH) and Tropica/Modern Aquarium (temperature).
